q: Photometry is the science of measuring visible light in units that are weighted according to the sensitivity of scientific instruments.
The human eye responds only to light having wavelengths between about 400 nm and 700 nm.
The human eye is most sensitive to blue light of 450 nm.
Luminous flux, luminous intensity, absorbance, and illuminance are the four main photometric quantities.
Luminous flux (luminous power) $\Phi$ is a measurement of the power of invisible light produced by a light source.
Luminous flux is measured in candela (cd).
One lumen (1 lm) is equivalent to 1 watt of light with a wavelength of 555 nm.
Luminous intensity $I_v$ is a measure of the luminous flux emitted by a light source in a direction per unit solid angle.
Luminous intensity is measured in lux (lx) or lumen per steradian.
The candela is the SI basic unit.
A solid angle $\Omega$ is expressed in radian (rad) in the SI system.
A steradian can be defined as the solid angle subtended at the centre of a unit sphere by a unit area on its surface.
The solid angle subtended at the centre by the whole surface area of the sphere is $2\pi$ steradian.
Luminance $L_v$ is the luminous intensity per unit volume of light travelling in a given direction.
The SI unit for luminance is candela per metre squared (cd m$^{\text{-}2}$).
Illuminance $E_v$ is the luminous intensity incident on a surface per unit area.
Illuminance is a measure of how much the incident light reflects off a surface.
The SI unit for illuminance is lux (lx).
1 lux (lx) is equivalent to 1 candela per square meter (cd m$^{\text{-}2}$).
According to the inverse square law of illuminance, the illuminance $E_v$ at any point on a surface is directly proportional to the square of the distance $r$ between the source and surface.
The inverse square law of illuminance applies only when the light falls normally on the surface ($\cos \theta = 1$).
The angle $\theta$ in the illuminance formula $E_v = \frac{I_v \cos \theta}{r^2}$ is between the direction of light and the illuminated surface itself.
The minimum average illuminance for school study halls should be 50 lux.
